alignments.candidate_evidence: Designs, specs and directs LLM coding agents; advanced Excel/Sheets; builds Looker dashboards
alignments.job_requirement: Use low-code/no-code tools to build automations/orchestrations (Workato, Okta Workflows, Zapier, etc.)

alignments.candidate_evidence: Designs, specs and directs LLM coding agents to build production systems; owns evaluation methodology, benchmarking and calibration
alignments.job_requirement: Leverage, design, implement AI with a focus on the LRC function

alignments.candidate_evidence: Proficient SQL, BigQuery querying; limited native API integration or infrastructure-as-code evidence
alignments.job_requirement: Work with APIs across major vendors (Okta, Google, GCP, Aliyun, etc.)

gaps.gap: Production software engineering, infrastructure as code (Terraform), and hands-on coding in Python or other languages at professional depth
gaps.mitigation: Candidate's Python is basic and data-analysis focused; no stated Terraform or IaC experience; role requires 'programming language, such as python, and infrastructure as code' as core duty

gaps.gap: Hands-on identity and access management (SAML, OAuth, SSO, RBAC, ABAC, PBAC, ACLs) at expert level
gaps.mitigation: No evidence of identity standards engineering or IAM system architecture; role explicitly requires 'expert level understanding' of these standards

gaps.gap: Deep orchestration and workflow platform expertise (Workato, Okta Workflows, etc.)
gaps.mitigation: Candidate has automation and agentic-tooling leadership but not demonstrated hands-on low-code/no-code platform administration or complex workflow design

gaps.gap: MDM, cloud infrastructure (AWS, GCP), and SaaS ecosystem administration
gaps.mitigation: No stated experience with Kandji, JAMF, Intune, or AWS/GCP operations; role requires strong understanding across these systems

gaps.gap: Core IT engineering and operations discipline
gaps.mitigation: Background is commercial operations and product transformation at Google; IT engineering, IT operations, and corporate IT engineering are distinct disciplines with different tooling, escalation models, and incident-response expectations

output.level_rationale: Role requires 5+ years relevant experience, expert IAM/orchestration depth, and full-stack IT engineering ownership across identity, workflows, cloud, and MDM systems. This maps to mid-to-senior IC (L5) scope within IT engineering discipline, with scope slightly elevated by global legal-team partnership and autonomy expectations.
output.summary: This is a Senior IT Engineer role focused on IT infrastructure, identity management, workflow automation, and legal-team support—a discipline fundamentally outside the candidate's eight-year history in commercial operations, product operations, and business transformation at Google. While the candidate demonstrates advanced skills in agentic AI, data querying, and cross-functional program delivery, the role requires hands-on production software engineering, infrastructure-as-code (Terraform), expert-level identity standards (SAML/OAuth/RBAC), and deep expertise in low-code orchestration platforms (Workato, Okta Workflows) and MDM systems—none of which are evident in the supplied profile. The role is within target seniority (L5) but is a poor fit for role family and discipline.
uncertainties: Airwallex company stage context not stated in JD; assuming high-growth scale-up (2,200+ people, $8B valuation) suggests established infrastructure, but exact IT maturity and team structure unclear
Extent of hands-on coding vs. direction-only in candidate's AI/agentic-tooling role not detailed; if primarily direction, engineering gap widens
No detail on candidate's exposure to identity/access-management architecture or corporate IT incident response
Exact scope of 'admin' role for Salesforce and degree of infrastructure or systems work involved not expanded
